purpose: Machine-readable map from exposed operation routes to their authoritative operation owner.

scope.route_granularity: Each listed callable symbol maps to exactly one owner. Route groups may use symbols_by_owner to keep nearby source symbols together while preserving one owner per symbol. Constants, pure data types, and generated schema descriptors are outside operation ownership unless they expose a callable boundary.

owners.description: Structured generated-protobuf operation request processing and its ProtoJSON request route.
Hash digest operations.
Constant-time equality and verification operations.
CSPRNG byte, nonce, and salt generation.
MAC tag generation.
MAC tag verification.
AEAD encryption/seal operations.
AEAD decryption/open operations.
AES-KW key wrapping.
AES-KW key unwrapping.
KDF output derivation across Argon2id, HKDF, PBKDF2, KMAC, and JWA Concat KDF.
Signature keypair generation for raw-key algorithms; signature owns Ed25519 and NIST/secp256k1 ECDSA, BIP-340 adds BIP-340 x-only keypairs, and post-quantum signature adds ML-DSA and SLH-DSA.

Signature keypair derivation/import from existing secret material; signature owns Ed25519 and NIST/secp256k1 ECDSA, BIP-340 adds BIP-340 x-only keypairs, and post-quantum signature adds ML-DSA and SLH-DSA.
Signature public-key derivation from private key material or handle.
Signature creation for Ed25519, NIST/secp256k1 ECDSA, ML-DSA, and SLH-DSA raw-key routes; BIP-340 has its dedicated BIP-340 route.
Signature verification for Ed25519, NIST/secp256k1 ECDSA, ML-DSA, and SLH-DSA raw-key routes; BIP-340 has its dedicated BIP-340 route.
BIP-340 Schnorr signing over 32-byte messages with explicit auxiliary randomness.
BIP-340 Schnorr verification over 32-byte messages and x-only public keys.
RSA PKCS#1 v1.5 and RFC 8017 RSA-PSS verification, including emLen = k - 1 moduli, with explicit hash, MGF1, salt-length, and DER public-key wrapper selection.
Key-agreement keypair generation for raw-key algorithms.
Key-agreement keypair derivation/import from existing secret material.
Raw key-agreement public-key derivation from private key material through the derive-keypair owner; platform handles use the separate platform-key lifecycle route.
ECDH/X25519 shared-secret derivation.
KEM keypair generation.
KEM keypair derivation/import from existing secret material.
KEM public-key derivation from private key material.
Randomized KEM encapsulation.
Deterministic test-vector KEM encapsulation.
KEM decapsulation.
HPKE KEM keypair generation.
HPKE KEM deterministic keypair derivation.
HPKE Base-mode seal.
HPKE Base-mode open.
HPKE sender exporter setup.
HPKE receiver exporter setup.
HPKE single-shot PSK-mode seal.
HPKE single-shot PSK-mode open.
Public-key byte, multicodec, multikey, JWK, and DER/JOSE conversion helpers.
Validated private-key import and encoding helpers for supported key formats.
ECDSA DER and JOSE signature conversion helpers.
Algorithm-selected public keypair and derive-keypair facade routes that dispatch to signature, key-agreement, or KEM semantic operations.

Provider-neutral platform-key handle encoding, key generation, public-key lookup, deletion, and explicit attestation evidence; Swift uses P-256 Secure Enclave handles and the Android AAR uses purpose-bound Android Keystore / StrongBox handles.
Handle-backed platform signature creation and verification; Swift uses P-256 Secure Enclave signing keys and the Android AAR uses P-256 Android Keystore / StrongBox signing keys.
Handle-backed platform key agreement; Swift P-256 Secure Enclave ECDH and Android Keystore / StrongBox P-256 ECDH are supported provider-specific owners outside the raw-key Rust transport.
Provider catalog, native loading, and package-provider support routes.
Argon2id profile selection and platform-capability policy helpers.
Best-effort managed-runtime memory clearing helpers.
Public SDK protobuf value conversion, wire-envelope, and result-wrapper helpers that do not invoke a cryptographic operation.
Typed error, status, and generated-protobuf adapter mapping.

root_only_symbols.reason: hash and constant-time moved hash selection into the main facade operation layer and removed the parallel published dispatch-crate hash selector.
MAC moved MAC selection into the main facade operation layer and removed the parallel published dispatch-crate MAC selector.
MAC moved MAC verification into the main facade operation layer and removed the parallel published dispatch-crate MAC selector.
AEAD moved AEAD seal selection into the main facade operation layer and removed the parallel published dispatch-crate AEAD selector.
AEAD moved AEAD open selection into the main facade operation layer and removed the parallel published dispatch-crate AEAD selector.

route_groups.path: crates/crypto/src/lib.rs; crates/crypto/src/aes.rs; crates/crypto/src/aes_kw.rs; crates/crypto/src/aes_gcm_siv.rs; crates/crypto/src/chacha20_poly1305.rs; crates/crypto/src/concat_kdf.rs; crates/crypto/src/ed25519.rs; crates/crypto/src/hmac.rs; crates/crypto/src/hkdf.rs; crates/crypto/src/hpke.rs; crates/crypto/src/key_agreement_error.rs; crates/crypto/src/kmac.rs; crates/crypto/src/ml_dsa_44.rs; crates/crypto/src/ml_dsa_65.rs; crates/crypto/src/ml_dsa_87.rs; crates/crypto/src/p256.rs; crates/crypto/src/p384.rs; crates/crypto/src/p521.rs; crates/crypto/src/pbkdf2.rs; crates/crypto/src/secp256k1.rs; crates/crypto/src/sha2.rs; crates/crypto/src/sha3.rs; crates/crypto/src/signature_error.rs; crates/crypto/src/slh_dsa.rs; crates/crypto/src/x25519.rs; crates/crypto/src/operations/hpke.rs; crates/crypto/src/operations/key_agreement.rs; crates/crypto/src/operations/signature.rs
route_groups.notes: The SHA-2, SHA-3, HMAC, AEAD, AES-KW, KDF, Ed25519, ECDSA, BIP-340, RSA, key-agreement, KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, and HPKE root modules are public facades over their operation-layer owners. JWK and JWK/Multikey conversion helpers are enumerated individually because they are callable public routes, not only data-model modules.

route_groups.excluded_public_symbols: Signer and verifier constructors, accessors, error predicates, and trait/type definitions establish or describe an adapter; they do not execute a cryptographic operation.

route_groups.source_symbol_note: symbols_by_owner records the actual JavaScript export names declared by wasm_bindgen js_name; Rust snake_case function identifiers are source implementation details, not exported route names.
route_groups.notes: AEAD exports route through the root AEAD facades and semantic operation owner while preserving the package's reviewed bundled RustCrypto provider selection. ML-DSA and SLH-DSA exports route through the root signature facades and semantic operation owner, validate fixed-size inputs before copying, and keep secret and message copies under zeroizing Rust ownership.

route_groups.excluded_public_members: Initializers, stored/computed properties, redacted descriptions, and pure data-model accessors do not execute an operation.
Provider constructors, stored/computed properties, redacted descriptions, enum cases, and pure data-model accessors do not execute an operation.

route_groups.symbol_format: Type.method or Type.method(parameter-labels) where an overload requires disambiguation

route_groups.excluded_public_members: Provider-catalog properties, error and data types, constructors, enum cases, and pure data accessors do not execute an operation. Public overloads are represented by parameter type or label where the base method name is ambiguous.

route_groups.excluded_public_reexports: Generated schema descriptors, generated enum values, and TypeScript-only data types re-exported from this module are not callable operation routes under scope.route_granularity.

route_groups.excluded_exported_helpers: Provider-catalog constants and validateBytes.ts byte-shape, overlap, ownership, and property-reading helpers do not execute cryptographic or boundary-conversion operations. The validation helpers are package-internal composition exports rather than package exports; all are outside operation ownership under scope.route_granularity.

completeness_rules: Every callable operation route in this ledger maps to exactly one owner id.
No callable route may remain an unsupported implementation placeholder; invalid algorithm or provider combinations return typed errors.
Generated support surfaces may use wildcard ownership entries only when they cannot execute cryptographic operations.
Secure Enclave and Android Keystore / StrongBox signing and ECDH routes are platform-key lifecycle owners, not raw-key signature or key-agreement owners.
Android raw-key route groups point to the shared Kotlin owners; Android-only hardware-key lifecycle methods are owned under packages/kotlin-android and remain separate from those raw-key routes.
